MCP Context Optimizer — v1.0.0-beta

BENCHMARK (100 calls, 20 unique tool invocations):
  Cache Hit Ratio : 80.0%
  Tokens Saved    : ~36,000
  Latency         : ~110ms (direct) → ~26ms (cached), 4x reduction

COMPONENTS:
  - Hybrid L1+L2 cache (LRU in-memory + SQLite persistence)
  - MCP Application Firewall (path traversal, prompt injection, oversized payload DLP)
  - Circuit Breaker with configurable thresholds
  - Request deduplication for concurrent identical calls
  - Real-time Web Dashboard (React/Vite, port 9090)
  - Admin HTTP API (stats, cache management, config inspection)

DISTRIBUTION:
  - Docker image: ghcr.io/maksboreichuk88-commits/mcp-server:latest
  - CI/CD: GitHub Actions — typecheck, build, Docker push on every commit to main
  - Integration examples: Claude Desktop, LangChain/Python

FIREWALL MODULE (src/middleware/firewall.ts):
  Blocks: /etc/, .env, .ssh/, /proc/ path traversal
  Blocks: prompt injection patterns ("ignore previous instructions", etc.)
  Blocks: payloads > 256KB (OOM protection)
  Warns:  tool names containing exec, eval, shell, subprocess

CHANGELOG:
  - feat: hybrid L1/L2 cache with selective caching policies (alwaysCacheTools / neverCacheTools)
  - feat: MCP Application Firewall — first DLP layer for MCP stdio traffic
  - feat: stdout buffering fix — no more JSON parse failures on large tool responses
  - feat: zombie process cleanup on proxy crash/exit
  - feat: React/Vite dashboard with real-time Recharts graphs
  - feat: --admin-only mode for standalone dashboard
  - feat: Dockerfile (multi-stage Alpine build)
  - feat: GitHub Actions CI/CD with Docker publish to ghcr.io
  - feat: integration examples for Claude Desktop and LangChain
  - feat: benchmark script (tests/benchmark.ts)
  - fix: tools/call no longer cached by default (prevents caching side effects)
  - fix: admin port validation, CORS, bearer auth middleware
